{{< include macros.qmd >}} ### Aim and Context Aim: Predict the thermal performance of (existing) buildings' walls in the presence of unknown thermal bridge effects. Context (United Kingdom) - UK decarbonisation policy legislates an 80\% reduction of its 1990 greenhouse gas emissions by 2050. - 45\% of all carbon emissions can be attributed to buildings. - 60\% of those emissions are associated space heating in domestic buildings. - It is estimated that up to 85\% of existing building stock will still be standing by 2050. - Effective large-scale retrofitting of the existing housing stock is vitally important to reach those low-carbon targets. - External walls comprise approximately 40\% of heat loss/gain through the building envelope ## Thermal Bridges - 3D heat transfer arising from heterogeneities and/or discontinuities in the thermophysical composition of the building fabric (e.g. from material defects/degradation) - Typically present in the existing housing stock, TBs can decrease up to 40\% the thermal efficiency of buildings - Can lead to moisture condensation which affects indoor air quality, and pose serious health hazards to occupants (e.g. 30-50\% increase in respiratory conditions and asthma). ![](Figs_New/Real2.png){width="65%"} ## Forward and Inverse Model $$ \red{c(\bfx)}\frac{\partial \blue{T(\bfx, t)}}{\partial t} =\nabla \cdot \red{k(\bfx)} \nabla \blue{T(\bfx, t)} $$ Convective BCs on the internal/external surfaces $\mathcal{S}_{\alpha},~(\alpha=I,E)$ $$ \begin{align*} - \red{k(\bfx)} \nabla T(\bfx, t)\cdot\mathbf{n}(\bfx)=\red{R_{\alpha}^{-1}}[T(\bfx,t)- \green{T_{\alpha}(t)}], \end{align*} $$ Adiabatic BCs on $\partial\Omega_{W}\setminus \Big(\mathcal{S}_{I}\cup\mathcal{S}_{E}\Big)$ $$ \begin{align*} - \alert{k(\bfx)} \nabla T(\bfx, t)\cdot\mathbf{n}(\bfx)=0 \end{align*} $$ Forward model \alert{inputs: } $$\alert{U(\bm{x})=(c(\bm{x}),k(\bm{x}),R_{I},R_{E}).}$$ Forward model \adv{outputs} : \\ $$ \adv{T(\bfx,t)\big\vert_{\mathcal{S}_{\alpha}}},\quad \text{and} \quad \adv{Q_{\alpha}(\bfx,t)\equiv -k(\bfx)\nabla T(\bfx,t)\big\vert_{\mathcal{S}_{\alpha}}} $$ ## oi ::: columns ::: {.column width="50%"} $$\nabla \cdot \color{red}{\kappa(x)} \color{black}{\nabla} \color{blue}{V(x)}\color{black}{=}I(x_{s^{+}},x_{s^{-}})$$ +BCs ::: ::: {.column width="50%"} ![](Figs/ERT2.png){width="65%"} ::: ::: ## Goal {.scrollable} ![](Figs/ERT1.png){width="100%"} ## Defectos ::: {layout-ncol="2"} ![](Figs/ICIAM2.jpg) ![](Figs/ICIAM3.jpg) ::: ## Problem Inverso en RTM [**Objectivo:**]{.alert} Inferir propiedades fisicas del material dado datos de presion durante la injeccion de resina
[**Application:**]{.alert} Control activo y evaluacion no destructiva (NDE) ::: columns ::: {.column width="70%"} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.column width="30%"} ::: ::: ## RTM -- Modelo ::: columns ::: {.column width="50%"} Darcy flow: $$ - \nabla \cdot \Big[ \frac{ \kappa(x)}{\mu} \nabla p(x,t) \Big] = 0,\quad \textrm{in}~ D(t) \nonumber $$ + ICs + BCs\\ Moving front: $x\in \Upsilon(t)$: $$ \frac{d \color{red}{\Upsilon(t)}}{dt}= - \frac{\kappa(x)}{\varphi(x) \mu} \nabla p(x,t) \cdot \mathbf{n}(x,t) \nonumber $$ ::: ::: {.column width="50%"} ::: ::: ## Goal 1: Characterisation of material properties (with uncertainty) ![](Figs/Goal1.png) ## Goal 2: Calibrated Model (ready for control) Needs deep learning surrogate! ![](Figures/Experiment4.png) ::: backhub ↩ Volver al inicio :::
